title: Feature: Declarative Swarm Agent Behavior — YAML-driven supervisor
description: Parse the 14 existing agent YAML definitions into AgentDefinition structs and drive all supervisor behavior from them: model selection, context assembly with L1/L2/L3 levels, TDD workflow phases, feedback loop gates, quality thresholds, and swarm composition.

phases.name: YAML Parsing
phases.description: Parse agent YAMLs into typed Rust structs

phases.name: Model + Context from YAML
phases.description: Replace hardcoded model selection and context assembly

phases.name: Workflow + Feedback from YAML
phases.description: TDD phases and compile/lint/test gates from YAML

phases.name: Swarm Composition
phases.description: Swarm definition as YAML with agent roles and objectives

phases.name: Integration
phases.description: Wire into supervisor, remove hardcoded behavior

steps.description: Add serde_yaml dependency to hex-cli (currently only toml is present). Define AgentDefinition, ModelConfig, ContextConfig (with LoadStrategy and TokenBudget), WorkflowConfig, FeedbackLoopConfig (with GateConfig), EscalationConfig, QualityThresholds, InputSpec, OutputSpec structs. All with Deserialize. IMPORTANT: Agent YAMLs have role-specific schemas — hex-coder uses workflow.phases[] + feedback_loop, planner uses workflow.steps[] + escalation. Use Option<> wrappers or a WorkflowVariant enum to handle both patterns. Validate against hex-coder.yml AND planner.yml as reference schemas.

Implement AgentDefinition::load_all() — reads all *.yml from hex-cli/assets/agents/hex/hex/ via rust-embed, parses each with serde_yaml, returns HashMap<String, AgentDefinition>. Handle parse errors gracefully (warn and skip malformed YAMLs). AgentDefinition::load(name) for loading a specific agent. Unit tests parsing hex-coder.yml.

Replace hardcoded model selection in supervisor with YAML-driven selection. Remove default_model_for() hardcoded mapping in model_selection.rs (lines 54-62) and replace with YAML lookup. Map YAML model names (sonnet/haiku/opus) to OpenRouter model IDs via a name→ID table. Implement upgrade logic: if iteration > threshold from YAML upgrade_condition, switch to upgrade_to model. Fallback chain: preferred → fallback → dev-pipeline.yml model_defaults → openrouter/free. NOTE: Until step-8 fully wires everything, both YAML and hardcoded paths may coexist — gate removal of hardcoded defaults behind a feature flag or keep old path as unreachable fallback until step-8 confirms all agents are YAML-driven.

Replace hardcoded context assembly with YAML load_strategy. Implement L1 (AST summary via hex summarize), L2 (function signatures only), L3 (full file content). Respect token_budget.allocation per category. Resolve {{target_adapter}} and {{current_edit_file}} placeholders from workplan step. Build AgentContext from YAML strategy instead of files_for_tier().

Implement YAML workflow phases in supervisor. For hex-coder: pre_validate (boundary check gate) → red (generate failing tests) → green (minimal implementation) → refactor (clean up) → test_coverage_gate. Each phase reads steps from YAML. Blocking gates prevent advancement. on_fail instructions passed to fixer.

Implement YAML feedback loop in supervisor. Read gates (compile/lint/test) from YAML with language-specific commands (tsc/eslint/vitest for TS, cargo check/clippy/test for Rust). Respect timeout_ms per gate. Use on_fail text as instructions for fixer context. Implement on_max_iterations escalation (switch to better model or report to coordinator).

Parse existing swarm composition YAMLs from hex-cli/assets/swarms/ (dev-pipeline.yml already exists with full schema: agents[], objectives[], iteration config, grade_thresholds). Already embedded via rust-embed (same pattern as agent YAMLs in hex-cli/assets/agents/). Parse into SwarmComposition struct matching the existing schema: agents with role/cardinality/tier_scope/inference/context/depends_on/parallel_with, objectives with evaluate/threshold, iteration limits. Supervisor reads this to configure the agent pipeline instead of hardcoded dispatch.

Wire YAML-driven behavior into Supervisor. Replace hardcoded execute_agent/dispatch_agent with YAML-driven dispatch: load agent def → select model from YAML → build context from YAML load_strategy → run workflow phases from YAML → apply feedback loop gates from YAML → check quality thresholds from YAML. Remove hardcoded match arms.

Quality thresholds from YAML. Read test_coverage, max_lint_warnings, max_file_lines, max_function_lines, max_cyclomatic_complexity from agent YAML. Apply in quality gate evaluation. Report which thresholds passed/failed. Different agents can have different thresholds (reviewer stricter than coder).
